name: 06_Sentiment_Analysis_SVM_Sentiment140_ML
query: Por favor, ayúdame a construir un sistema para el análisis de sentimientos en tweets utilizando el conjunto de datos Sentiment140 disponible en Hugging Face. Carga el conjunto de datos Sentiment140 y, al cargar los datos, límpialos eliminando palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`. Usa Word2Vec o GloVe para la vectorización de texto. Esto debe ocurrir en `src/data_loader.py`. Luego, implementa y entrena un clasificador SVM en `src/model.py`. Finalmente, escribe un informe de la precisión del clasificador en `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`. Idealmente, el informe debe ser fácilmente interpretable.

requirements.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Sentiment140", disponible en "Hugging Face", se obtiene en `src/data_loader.py`.
El conjunto de datos se limpia, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`.
Se utilizan incrustaciones de palabras, ya sea "Word2Vec" o "GloVe", para convertir texto en vectores en `src/data_loader.py`.
Un "clasificador SVM" se implementa y entrena en `src/model.py`.
La puntuación de precisión se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

preferences.criteria: El proceso de descarga del conjunto de datos debe ser confiable, con un manejo claro de errores.
El informe final de precisión debe ser directo y fácil de interpretar.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Sentiment140", disponible en "Hugging Face", se obtiene en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/data_loader.py` file contains a placeholder function `load_dataset()` that does not include any actual implementation for obtaining the "Sentiment140" dataset from "Hugging Face". Although the trajectory evidence indicates that the dataset was eventually downloaded successfully, the code provided does not demonstrate this functionality.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos se limpia, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement specifies that the dataset should be cleaned by removing stop words, punctuation, and special characters within `src/data_loader.py`. However, the provided content of `data_loader.py` does not show any implementation related to data cleaning processes such as removing stop words, punctuation, or special characters. Additionally, the trajectory evidence indicates that there was a syntax error affecting the data cleaning functionality, and even after correction, a warning was issued about the use of an obsolete function for removing special characters. This suggests that the cleaning process may not be fully implemented or reliable.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utilizan incrustaciones de palabras, ya sea "Word2Vec" o "GloVe", para convertir texto en vectores en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement specifies that word embeddings, either "Word2Vec" or "GloVe", should be used to convert text to vectors in `src/data_loader.py`. Although the trajectory evidence indicates that "GloVe" was successfully used for text conversion, the provided content of `data_loader.py` does not show any implementation of word embedding conversion. The file contains only placeholder functions without actual code for loading datasets or converting text to vectors using embeddings.

judge_stats.criteria: Un "clasificador SVM" se implementa y entrena en `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The content of `src/model.py` provided does not show any implementation of an SVM classifier. The file contains a placeholder function `build_model()` that does not implement or train an SVM classifier. Although the trajectory evidence indicates that the SVM classifier was eventually trained successfully, the actual code evidence from `src/model.py` does not demonstrate this implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: La puntuación de precisión se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The content of `accuracy_score.txt` is shown as `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which indicates that the actual accuracy score is not present in the file. Although the trajectory evidence mentions that the issue with permissions was resolved and the file was saved correctly, the placeholder content suggests that the accuracy score was not properly printed or saved in the file.
